release_body:
Version 1.0.0

New, potentially _breaking_, feature is that we do not modify or clone objects set after construction, as we did before. The following will throw `AttributeError`

```Python
a = Dict()
a.a = {}
a.a.b = 2  # throws AttributeError as a.a is a dict (and not an addict)
```

For this functionality you should rather do

```Python
a = Dict()
a.a = Dict()
a.a.b = 2  # works
```

Items set through the constructor will be cloned (as before)

```Python
a = Dict({'a': {'b': 2}})
a.a.c = 3  # works
```

-- 
Mats

pytypes 1.0 beta 2
===============

The second release of pytypes is mainly a bugfix release.
The most notable new feature is better handling of type variables.
pytypes 1.0 beta 2 is available on [PyPI](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/pytypes).

Enjoy!

# Installation instructions:

Plugin:
---

Install zip attached file.

Web service:
---

http://fanficfare.appspot.com/

Command line interface:
---
Rather being delivered as a zip file with dependencies included, FanFicFare is delivered as a `pip` Python package.  It's been tested on Python 2.6 and 2.7, but not on Python 3.

`pip install FanFicFare`
or
`pip install --upgrade FanFicFare`
...to update.

(pip will automatically install dependencies beautifulsoup4, html5lib, chardet and six.)

Run `fanficfare -h` from command-line.

First stable release!
No breaking changes - only bug fixes.

For all changes, please see the [release notes](https://github.com/Azure/azure-uamqp-python/blob/master/HISTORY.rst).

Changes
------------

- Fixed race-condition for iterating addresses from the DNSCache. (#2620)
- Fix docstring for request.host (#2591)
- Fix docstring for request.remote (#2592)
Release of version 20180110
* Fix issue with looping clones
Release of version 20180225

Breaking changes:

* *successful_requests()* must now be explicitly enabled before use.
* *DynamicTimeout* retries parameter has been removed (redundant).
* Redirects are no longer followed by default, the *FollowRedirects* heuristic must be used.
* *Entry* is no longer a named tuple, so it can no longer be used as a tuple and *_replace* is no longer available.

Improvements:

* New event is available in heuristics. *on_request_successful* is triggered after the request is complete, after the lock has been released.
* Upon temporary failures, retries are requeued rather than requested immediately.

Bug fixes:

* Memory usage is no longer growing if *successful_requests()* is not used.
